titleStmt.title: Letter on the death of Captain Charles M. Celthurst Vesey and the vacancy of Deputy Lieutenants for Dublin, Jan. 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, Lord Lieutenant of County Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to the Under Secretary at Dublin Castle ( Sir Matthew Nathan) with regards to the death of Captain Charles M. Celthurst Vesey and the resulting vacancy of Deputy Lieutenant for Dublin from Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, Lord Lieutenant of County Dublin. Meath proposes the Viscount Powerscourt for the position.

correspAction.persName: Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, Lord Lieutenant of County Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
January [11.] 1916 Sir As there is a vacancy in the list of Deputy Lieutenants for
the County of Dublin, owing to the death of [ Captain Charles M.
Colthurst Vesey, ] I propose, subject to the approval of the Lord
Lieutenant, under the provisions of the Militia Act 1882,
to issue my Commission to,
[ The Viscount Powerscourt M.V.O., name
Powerscourt, Enniskerry, address Co.Wicklow. Ireland. ] I shall be obliged if you will submit this letter to His
Excellency, and, should he approve of the appointment, if you
will forward me the usual form of Commission. Believe me
Yours very truly Meath
H.M.L. for County and City of Dublin. The Under Secretary
Dublin Castle.
